title: salix × ambigua nothosubsp. maritima
intro: Salix × ambigua nothosubsp. maritima, also known as the coastal willow, is a hybrid plant species that is a combination of Salix fragilis and Salix × septentrionalis. The coastal willow has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Salix × ambigua nothosubsp. maritima include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and act as a natural antiseptic, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health concerns. The plant can be prepared in various herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
